steps.title: Put the equation in quadratic form
Use the two-real-solutions condition
steps.content:
A quadratic has two distinct real solutions when the discriminant is positive:
$$b^2-4ac\gt0$$
Here $a=k-24$, $b=-16$, and $c=8$.

steps.title: Choose the greatest integer
steps.content: Since $k$ is an integer and must be less than $32$, the greatest possible value is 31. Values $32$ or greater make the discriminant zero or negative, so they do not produce two distinct real solutions.
